film.title: Midnight Cowboy
film.overview: Joe Buck is a wide-eyed hustler from Texas hoping to score big with wealthy New York City women; he finds a companion in Enrico "Ratso" Rizzo, an ailing swindler with a bum leg and a quixotic fantasy of escaping to Florida.

dialogues.dialogue: You're not looking for the Statue of Liberty at all.  No, ma'am, I ain't.  Why, you ought to be ashamed of yourself.  Come on, Baby. Do it for mama.  Come on, Baby. Mama's tired.

Preparing for this brief moment of glory at halftime.  A basic training as rigorous as the star football players themselves.  Long hours of precision choreography that would put the Rockettes to shame.  Musical arrangements, rehearsals, costume fittings.  A production in Hollywood's finest tradition.
dialogues.cause_raw: not being as rigorous in choreography

dialogues.dialogue: Remington shotgun for only $72.47.  You look real nice, loverboy. Real nice.  Make your old grandma proud.  You're gonna be the best-looking cowboy in the whole parade.  You'll be the best-looking one there.
My ideal is Gary Cooper, but he's dead.  Thank you. And, ma'am, what is your ideal in a man?  A man who takes pride in his appearance.  Kindly consideration, first.  Tall. Definitely tall.
